meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics for Students: Solve Slow Dev Speed (Beginner Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your app development is slow. Learn how to implement analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your student projects.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from building features users don't need. By integrating lightweight analytics early, you gain data-driven visibility into user behavior. Use Antigravity to scaffold event tracking and Claude Code to automate implementation, allowing you to prioritize high-impact features and ship your MVP with confidence and speed.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect UI, yet your development velocity feels like wading through concrete. You are building in the dark, guessing what your users want, and wasting precious hours on features that nobody actually clicks. The frustration of 'building for nobody' is the silent killer of every student project.

But the best engineers don't guess—they observe. By integrating a lean analytics layer into your stack, you transform your app from a black box into a feedback engine. This isn't just about metrics; it's about reclaiming your time and building a portfolio piece that demonstrates real-world engineering maturity.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three key actions that define success in your app. This prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Product Architect. Help me define 3 core event-tracking metrics for a student SaaS project. Focus on user engagement and conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose a tracking schema for my app based on these 3 metrics'

steps.title: Scaffold the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Initialize your analytics provider within your base layout component to ensure global availability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable Analytics wrapper component for React that supports event logging with types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an analytics service file and inject it into the main App layout'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Attach event tracking to your primary call-to-action buttons to measure conversion rates immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom hook useTrackEvent that simplifies logging events to my provider with standardized metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor all button components to include the useTrackEvent hook on click'

steps.title: Automate Error Reporting
steps.description: Slow development often comes from hidden bugs. Capture runtime errors automatically to debug faster.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an error boundary component that automatically logs exceptions to my analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap the application router in an ErrorBoundary and configure it to report to the analytics service'

steps.title: Verify Data Flow
steps.description: Ensure your events are actually firing correctly before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that simulates a user journey and verifies that all tracking events are triggered correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run the verification script and log any missing tracking events found'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus on 'High-Intent' events to keep your dashboard clean and your focus sharp.
Use custom user properties to segment your data by student vs. pro users, even in your early MVP.
Implement 'Event Debouncing' on search inputs to prevent overwhelming your analytics API during testing.
Always include a 'development mode' flag to prevent your own testing sessions from skewing real user data.
Treat your analytics dashboard like a code repository; document every event name in a central README.
resources: Analytics Best Practices for SaaS: https://analytics-handbook.com
content.next_steps: Once your events are firing, wait for 50 unique users to interact with your app. Then, use that data to cut the features that have 0% engagement, significantly reducing your maintenance burden.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to stop guessing and start scaling. By treating data as a first-class citizen in your development process, you're already ahead of 90% of your peers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
